title: bistorta major subsp. carnea
intro: Bistorta major subsp. carnea, also known as the crimson bistort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Bistorta major subsp. carnea can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health and support the immune system.
